stories.headline: Treasuries Pare Drop in Oil-Driven Move Tied to Iran War
stories.summary: Treasuries pared declines, pulling two-year yields back from a more than 15-month high, as reports of a ceasefire between Israel and Iran eased the upward pressure on oil prices.

stories.headline: Armenia's pro-West government wins election despite Russian pressure

stories.headline: Jailed crypto founder Sam Bankman-Fried seeks Trump pardon

stories.headline: Peru election result close as vote counting continues
stories.summary: The race between right-wing Keiko Fujimori and left-wing Roberto Sánchez has been dominated by concerns over crime and political instability.

stories.headline: Zepto Publicly Files for Planned $1 Billion India IPO
stories.summary: Rapid-commerce firm Zepto Ltd. has filed an updated draft prospectus for an initial public offering, marking a key step toward a potential listing of one of India’s fastest-growing consumer internet companies.

stories.headline: BofA Warns It’s Time to ‘Take Profits’ as Red Flags Multiply
stories.summary: Investors should exercise caution regarding US stocks as an increasing number of “bear market signposts” point to an approaching top, according to Bank of America Securities.

stories.headline: Mercuria’s First-Half Profit Jumped 88% on Commodity Shocks
stories.summary: Mercuria Energy Group enjoyed an 88% jump in first-half profit, putting it on track for one of its best-ever annual results as the Hormuz crisis creates huge opportunities for commodity traders.

stories.headline: Giant Oil Supertanker Orders Eclipse Record Set in 2008
stories.summary: The world’s shipowners have placed orders for a record number of new oil supertankers, surpassing a boom back in 2008 that ultimately led to a glut and a collapse in rates.

stories.headline: Ken Paxton’s Former Defense Lawyer Endorses James Talarico
stories.summary: The lawyer, Dan Cogdell, helped save Mr. Paxton from criminal charges and an impeachment, but now he says the Texas attorney general has “lost sight of his mission.”

stories.headline: Judge Throws Out Policy Imposing $100,000 Fees for Skilled Worker Visas
stories.summary: The ruling voided “in its entirety” a policy from September requiring companies to pay $100,000 fees for H1-B visa petitions.

stories.headline: Robert Coles, Pulitzer-Winning Child Psychiatrist, Is Dead at 97
stories.summary: His five-volume “Children of Crisis” series, published between 1967 and 1977, drew on his conversations with American children whose voices were not often heard.

stories.summary: The split illustrates deep political polarisation in the South American country.

France’s justice minister refuses to resign after outcry over girl’s killing
stories.summary: Gérald Darmanin will not resign, but he apologised for the handling of the primary suspect in an 11-year-old girl's murder and asked France's public prosecutors to re-examine the country's 70,000 legal complaints under review for alleged crimes against children.

stories.headline: Stock market jitters remain amid tech fears and renewed Middle East attacks

stories.headline: Blue Owl Fund Hit by Redemptions Sells $500 Million Bonds

stories.headline: Amazon to Raise C$14 Billion in Record Canadian Dollar Bond Sale
stories.summary: Amazon.com Inc. is set to sell C$14 billion ($10 billion) of investment-grade bonds, the largest corporate bond offering on record in the currency, in a deal that drew around twice that amount in demand.

stories.headline: Millennium’s Grunfeld Says Schonfeld Trying to ‘Coerce’ Move
stories.summary: Millennium Management portfolio manager Adam Grunfeld asked a New York judge to throw out a suit filed against him by Schonfeld Strategic Advisors, saying the rival hedge fund group was essentially trying to pressure him to join.

stories.headline: Insecurity and instability drive voters in Peru's tight presidential race
stories.summary: After eight presidents in 10 years, many voters are looking for stability so the next president can focus on tackling crime and inequality.

stories.headline: One killed and dozens injured in Iranian drone strikes on Kuwait airport
stories.summary: Iran says the attack on Kuwait was in retaliation for earlier US strikes on an Iranian oil tanker and island.

stories.headline: Indonesia's Prabowo fires head of free meals scheme plagued by poisonings
